answer:
The fully interconnected network has \(\frac{n(n-1)}{2}\) links and a worst-case path length of 1, so its figure of merit is \(\frac{n(n-1)}{2} \times 1 = \frac{n(n-1)}{2}\).  
The ring network has \(n\) links and a worst-case path length of \(\frac{n}{2}\) (for even \(n\)), so its figure of merit is \(n \times \frac{n}{2} = \frac{n^2}{2}\).  
The ratio of the fully interconnected figure to the ring figure is \(\frac{n(n-1)/2}{n^2/2} = \frac{n-1}{n}\), which corresponds to option A.

\boxed{A}
